findings.description: Tabular data is rendered as a single continuous string of text without table structure.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, Appendix A is a block of comma-separated text that represents data rows. In the HTML, this text is rendered as a single wrapping paragraph without preserving the original line breaks or structure.

findings.description: Hierarchical chart is completely missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows a flowchart/diagram depicting the relationships between different Record Types (Record Type 1 pointing to 2, 7, etc.). The HTML completely omits this diagram.

findings.description: Multi-column data layout is lost, rendered as a single column.
findings.evidence: On page 8 of the PDF, the 'Type of material' section (under 'Bottom Composition') has two columns of codes (A=Dome, J=Reef Balls, etc.). The HTML renders this as a single column list within the table cell.

findings.description: Multi-column data layout is lost, rendered as a single column.
findings.evidence: On page 9 of the PDF, the 'Weather Element' and 'Wind Direction' sections have multiple columns of codes. The HTML renders these as single vertical lists within the table cells.

findings.description: The HTML heading hierarchy skips h1 and h2 levels entirely.
findings.evidence:
The document begins with

Division of Marine Fisheries

and uses

for all subsequent section headings (e.g.,

Appendix A

), with no

or

elements present.

findings.description: A flowchart diagram was flattened into plain text, losing its visual structure.
findings.evidence:
In the PDF Appendix B, there is a visual diagram showing hierarchical relationships between Record Types using connected boxes. In the HTML, this is rendered as a single flat paragraph: '

Environmental Data Record Type 1 Gear Data Record Type 7 Replicate Data...

'.

findings.description: Minor character translation artifacts present for degree symbols and apostrophes.
findings.evidence: In the Appendix C table, '°C' is rendered as '☐C' for Surface and Bottom Temperature. Additionally, on the final row, 'Type II's' is rendered as 'Type II☐s'.
